leadText: Hurricane (Japanese: ぼうふう Windstorm) is a damage-dealing Flying-type move introduced in Generation V. It is: TR89 in Pokémon Sword and Shield. TM160 in Pokémon Scarlet and Violet. TM079 in Pokémon Legends: Z-A.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation V 1.2 Generation VI onwards 1.3 Generation VIII 1.3.1 Pokémon Legends: Arceus 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation V [ edit | edit source ] Hurricane deals damage and has a 30% chance of confusing the target. Hurricane can hit non-adjacent targets in Triple Battles . Hurricane can hit Pokémon during the semi-invulnerable turns of Fly , Bounce , and Sky Drop . When used during rain , Hurricane bypasses accuracy checks to always hit, unless the target is in the semi-invulnerable turn of a move such as Dig or Dive . When used during harsh sunlight , it has an accuracy of 50%. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Hurricane's power changed from 120 to 110. Hurricane can be used as part of a Contest Spectacular combination , with the user gaining an extra three appeal points if the move Rain Dance was used in the prior turn. Hurricane is a wind move . Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Arceus [ edit | edit source ] Hurricane's power has been lowered to 100. It also can no longer confuse the target, since the status condition is not present in this game. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 5 Agile Strong Power 100 80 120 Accuracy 75% 75% 90% Action speed modifier Self 0 0 +5 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect ? ? ? ? ? ? None Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Hurricane functions similarly to Thunder and Blizzard (coincidentally all moves that have 70% accuracy in the turn-based games). Upon the wind-up completing a small whirlwind effect appears underneath the target's current location, which intensifies into a damaging windstorm after a delay. Due to how Bounce and Fly function in Z-A, Hurricane has no effect against targets using those moves while they are not visible on the screen. Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Special Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power 110 132 Cooldown 10s Move wind-up time 30 frames Effect duration —s Additional effect Chance 30% — — May confuse enemies struck

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description B W B2 W2 The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind that flies up into the sky. It may also confuse the target. X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind that flies up into the sky. This may also confuse the target. LA The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind. S V The user attacks by wrapping its opponent in a fierce wind. This may also confuse the target. ZA The user attacks by wrapping targets in a fierce wind. This may also leave targets confused.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD GtI It damages an enemy. It can hit an enemy in the air using a move such as Fly. It could also cause the Confused status. The Confused status impairs its sense of direction. S MD It damages an enemy in the air using a move such as Fly. It could also make the enemy confused. During rain or heavy rain, it never misses. But if it's sunny or in harsh sunlight, it's hard to land this move.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Both Hurricane and Thunder never miss during rain . This may be a relation to a thunderstorm. Hurricane will not lift Dugtrio or Diglett off the ground in the Generation VI animation like it does with other Pokémon.
